title: Battle of Raszyn
description: War of the Fifth Coalition. armies of the Austrian Empire under Archduke Ferdinand Karl Joseph of Austria-Este defeated the Duchy of Warsaw under Józef Antoni Poniatowski

title: Napoleon officially marries Marie Louise, Duchess of Parma.
description: This marriage was intended to secure an alliance between France and Austria and produce an heir for Napoleon.

title: Napoleon's abdication
description: In the night of 5-6 April, after long attempts at negotiations with the allies, Napoleon accepted defeat and wrote out a short text declaring that he had abdicated

title: Joseph Bonaparte becomes King of Naples
description: Joseph Bonaparte, Napoleon's older brother, is crowned King of Naples.

title: Battle of Smoliani
description: The Russians under General Peter Wittgenstein engaged the French forces of Marshal Claude Victor and Marshal Nicholas Oudinot.

title: Battle of Landshut
description: The Battle of Landshut was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Eggmühl
description: The Battle of Eggmühl was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Teugn-Hausen
description: The Battle of Teugn-Hausen was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Thann
description: The Battle of Thann was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Pfaffenhausen
description: The Battle of Pfaffenhausen was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Günzburg
description: The Battle of Günzburg was a battle of the War of the Fifth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of La Rothière
description: The Battle of La Rothière was a battle of the War of the Sixth Coalition in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Corunna
description: The Battle of Corunna was a battle of the Peninsular War in which the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte defeated the British army under Arthur Wellesley.

title: Battle of Copenhagen
description: The Battle of Copenhagen was a Naval battle of the Napoleonic Wars in which the British Royal Navy under the command of Admiral Sir Hyde Parker, with Horatio Nelson commanding the van, defeated the Danish-Norwegian fleet.

title: Battle of Sabugal
description: The Battle of Sabugal was a battle of the Peninsular War in which the British army under Arthur Wellesley defeated the French army under Marshal Masséna.

title: Battle of Fuentes de Oñoro
description: The Battle of Fuentes de Oñoro was a battle of the Peninsular War in which the British army under Arthur Wellesley defeated the French army under Marshal Masséna.

title: Siege of Almeida
description: The Siege of Almeida was a siege of the Peninsular War in which the British army under Arthur Wellesley defeated the French army under Marshal Masséna.

title: Battle of Almeida
description: The Battle of Fuentes de Oñoro was a battle of the Peninsular War in which the British army under Arthur Wellesley defeated the French army under Marshal Masséna.

title: Siege of Ferrara
description: The Siege of Ferrara was part of the Neapolitan War in which the Austrian army under the command of Lieutenant Field Marshal Friedrich Bianchi defeated the Neapolitan army under the command of Joachim Murat.

title: Battle of Tolentino
description: The Battle of Tolentino was part of the Neapolitan War in which the Austrian army under the command of Lieutenant Field Marshal Friedrich Bianchi defeated the Neapolitan army under the command of Joachim Murat.

title: Battle of Magnano
description: The Battle of Magnano was part of the War of the Second Coalition in which the Austrian army under the command of Lieutenant Field Marshal Friedrich Bianchi defeated the Neapolitan army under the command of Joachim Murat.

title: Battle of Trebbia
description: The Battle of Trebbia was part of the War of the Second Coalition in which the Austrian army under the command of Lieutenant Field Marshal Friedrich Bianchi defeated the Neapolitan army under the command of Joachim Murat.

title: Storming of Badajoz
description: The Storming of Badajoz was part of the Peninsular War in which the British army under Arthur Wellesley defeated the French army under Marshal Masséna.

title: Siege of Ciudad Rodrigo
description: The siege of Ciudad Rodrigo was the successful investment of the French-occupied city of Ciudad Rodrigo by Lord Wellington's Anglo-Portuguese Army from 7–20 January 1812.

title: Beginning of the Italian Campaign
description: Napoleon begins his offensive against the Austrian army.

title: Battle of Marengo
description: The Battle of Marengo was a battle of the War of the Second Coalition in which First Consul Napoleon Bonaparte and Austrian forces near the city of Alessandria, in Piedmont, Italy. Near the end of the day, the French overcame General Michael von Melas's surprise attack, drove the Austrians out of Italy

title: Battle of Montenotte
description: Napoleon's first victory in his first Italian campaign.

title: Battle of Toulouse
description: Wellington defeats Soult in France, unaware that Napoleon has already abdicated.

title: Battle of Millesimo
description: The Battle of Millesimo, fought on 13 and 14 April 1796, was the name that Napoleon Bonaparte gave in his correspondence to one of a series of small battles that were fought in Liguria, Northern Italy.

title: Second Battle of Dego
description: The Second Battle of Dego was fought on 14 and 15 April 1796 during the French Revolutionary Wars between French forces and Austro-Sardinian forces. The battle was fought near Dego, a hamlet in northwestern Italy, and ended in a French victory.

title: Battle of Mount Tabor
description: Battle between French forces commanded by Napoleon Bonaparte and General Jean-Baptiste Kléber, against an Ottoman Army under Abdullah Pasha al-Azm, ruler of Damascus.

title: Siege of Genoa begins
description: The siege of Genoa saw an Austrian army led by Michael von Melas besiege the port of Genoa, which was occupied by a French garrison under the command of André Massena.

title: Storming of Ratisbon
description: Also called the Battle of Regensburg, was fought between the army of the French Empire, led by Napoleon I, and that of the Austrian Empire, led by Archduke Charles.

title: Battle of Vauchamps
description: Napoleon's last major victory of the campaign.

title: Armistice of Cherasco
description: Signed between Victor Amadeus III of Sardinia and Napoleon Bonaparte. It withdrew Sardinia from the War of the First Coalition

title: End of the Peninsular War
description: Official conclusion of fighting in the Peninsula.

title: Battle of Cassano
description: The clash is part of the battle of the Adda River or the so-called forcing of the Adda, which on the first day resulted in a minor victory for the Russians under the Count Suvorov over Barthélemy Schérer's French forces at Lecco.

title: Battle of Landshut
description: Napoleon's capture of a major Austrian supply center.

title: Battle of Alcolea
description: This was the first pitched battle in Andalusia between a more or less organized Spanish army and French forces.

title: Acción de la Fuensanta
description: La acción de la Fuensanta fue un combate llevado a cabo el 12 de noviembre de 1810 entre las tropas de Pedro Villacampa y las del general Józef Chlopicki en el Santuario de la Fuensanta de Villel.

title: Acción de Orgaz
description: Enfrentó a una columna del Tercer Ejército español compuesta por unos ciento veinte jinetes y doscientos infantes, al mando del coronel Marcelino Villa, con una fuerza francesa de unos ochocientos jinetes pertenecientes a unidades de húsares y cazadores a caballo.

title: Batalla de Alcañiz
description: Tuvo lugar en la localidad turolense de Alcañiz el 23 de mayo de 1809 y donde las tropas españolas al mando de Joaquín Blake vencieron a las francesas comandadas por Louis Gabriel Suchet..

title: Batalla de Usagre
description: La batalla de Usagre se desarrolló el 25 de mayo de 1811 en Usagre, España, y enfrentó la caballería francesa del general de Latour-Maubourg a la caballería anglo-hispano-portuguesa del mayor general William Lumley. El enfrentamiento terminó con una victoria de los aliados hispano-anglo-portugueses.

title: Batalla del Bidasoa
description: La batalla de Bidasoa, también conocida como batalla de Larrún, fue una batalla librada el 7 de octubre de 1813 entre el ejército francés comandado por el mariscal Soult y el ejército aliado anglo-hispano-portugués comandado por el duque de Wellington. La batalla terminó con una victoria aliada.

title: Batalla del Puente de Triana
description: La batalla del Puente de Triana tuvo lugar el 27 de agosto de 1812 para poder tomar la ciudad de Sevilla.

title: Batalla de Cabezón
description: El 12 de junio de 1808 se produjo el primer intento del general García de la Cuesta de cortar las comunicaciones entre Madrid e Irún, cuando se produjeron sublevaciones en España tras el levantamiento del 2 de mayo.

title: Sitio de Cádiz
description: El asedio de Cádiz es como se le conoce al sitio de Cádiz y San Fernando que tuvo lugar el 5 de febrero de 1810, estableciendo un cerco a la ciudad de Cádiz y San Fernando hasta el 24 de agosto de 1812.

title: Batalla de Arroyomolinos
description: On 28 October 1811 an allied force under General Rowland Hill trapped and defeated a French force under General Jean-Baptiste Girard.

title: Batalla de Medina de Rioseco
description: El 14 de julio de 1808 en las inmediaciones de Medina de Rioseco (actualmente en la provincia de Valladolid) se enfrentaron el Ejército español comandado conjuntamente por los generales españoles García de la Cuesta y Joaquín Blake frente al mariscal Jean-Baptiste Bessières que término con el triunfo francés.

title: Toma del Fuerte del Rastrillar de Laredo
description: Fue una rápida operación, lograda en apenas dos días debido a la baja moral de los defensores italianos, aliados de Francia. Tras amenazar con no dar cuartel, las tropas españolas forzaron la rendición cuando los soldados italianos apresaron a sus oficiales franceses, facilitando la conquista con bajas mínimas.

title: Batalla de Fuentes de Oñoro
description: Entre el 3 y el 5 de mayo de 1811 se enfrentó el ejército Británico-Portugués con el ejército francés para disminuir la presión del sitio de la ciudad de Almeida.

title: Batalla de Roliça
description: 17 August 1808, was the first battle fought by the British army during the Peninsular War.

title: Batalla de Vimieiro
description: On 21 August 1808, the British under General Arthur Wellesley (who later became the Duke of Wellington) defeated the French under Major-General Jean-Andoche Junot near the village of Vimeiro.

title: Segona Batalla de Porto
description: El 12 de maig de 1809 va ser una contesa que va acabar amb la victòria per a l'exèrcit anglo-portuguès comandat per Sir Arthur Wellesley sobre l'exèrcit francès del mariscal Nicolas Jean de Dieu Soult.

title: Batalla de García Hernández
description: Fue una acción militar librada el 23 de julio de 1812 entre la caballería ligera del ejército británico y la caballería francesa.

title: Batalla de Majadahonda
description: The confrontation took place on August 11, 1812, where Napoleonic troops led by General Anne-François-Charles Trelliard defeated Anglo-Portuguese forces led by George Bock and Benjamin d’Urban.

title: Batalla de Caldiero
description: Fought on November 12th 1796 it was the first defeat Bonaparte suffered in open field. But his victory at Arcole just five days later help to minimize this defeat.

title: Coronation of Emperor Alexander I
description: The coronation of Emperor Alexander I and Elizabeth Alexeyevna took place on 15 September 1801 in the Dormition Cathedral of the Moscow Kremlin.

title: Battle of Aspern-Essling
description: The Battle of Aspern-Essling was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars, fought on May 21–22, 1809, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen. It marked Napoleon's first personal defeat in a major battle, as the Austrians successfully defended their ground and forced the French to retreat across the Danube River.

title: Battle of Wagram
description: The Battle of Wagram was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars, fought on July 5–6, 1809, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Leipzig
description: Known as the Battle of the Nations, was the largest battle in the Napoleonic Wars. Napoleon's defeat by a coalition of Austrian, Prussian, Russian, and Swedish forces led to his first exile.

title: Battle of Borodino
description: It was the bloodiest single-day battle in the Napoleonic Wars. Though technically a French victory, the massive losses weakened Napoleon's Grande Armée and foreshadowed the disastrous retreat from Moscow.

title: Battle of Corunna / Elviña
description: The Battle of Corunna (or A Coruña, La Corunna, La Coruña or La Corogne), in Spain known as Battle of Elviña, took place on 16 January 1809, when a French corps under Marshal of the Empire Jean de Dieu Soult attacked a British army under Lieutenant-General Sir John Moore. The battle took place during the Peninsular War, which was part of the wider Napoleonic Wars.

title: Battle of the Pyramids
description: It was a decisive French victory during Napoleon's Egyptian campaign. Napoleon famously addressed his troops: 'Soldiers, from the height of these pyramids, forty centuries look down upon you.'

title: Battle of Waterloo
description: The Battle of Waterloo on June 18, 1815 was Napoleon's final defeat. The Duke of Wellington and Prussian Marshal Blücher's combined forces ended Napoleon's rule as Emperor of France. The Lion's Mound memorial overlooks the battlefield.

title: Battle of Jena
description: Napoleon crushed the Prussian army at the Battle of Jena-Auerstedt. This decisive victory led to the French occupation of most of Prussia and cemented Napoleon's control over Central Europe.

title: Battle of Arcole
description: The Battle of Arcole or Battle of Arcola (15–17 November 1796) was fought between French and Austrian forces 25 kilometres (16 mi) southeast of Verona during the War of the First Coalition, a part of the French Revolutionary Wars.

title: Battle of Arcis-sur-Aube
description: The Battle of Arcis-sur-Aube (20-21 March 1814) saw an Imperial French army under Napoleon face a much larger Allied army led by Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg during the War of the Sixth Coalition.

title: Battle of Auerstaedt
description: This battle pitted the first Prussian army under King Frederick William III of Prussia against part of the French army led by Louis Nicolas Davout on October 14, 1806, parallel to the Battle of Jena, which was fought on the same date.

title: Battle of Bautzen
description: The Battle of Bautzen (20-21 May 1813) saw an Imperial French army under Napoleon face a much larger Allied army led by Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg during the War of the Sixth Coalition.

title: Battle of Wagram
description: The Battle of Wagram was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars, fought on July 5–6, 1809, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Ulm
description: The Battle of Ulm was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars, fought on October 16–19, 1805, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Austrian army under Archduke Charles Louis, Duke of Teschen.

title: Battle of Trafalgar
description: The Battle of Trafalgar was fought on October 21, 1805, between the British Royal Navy and the combined fleets of the French and Spanish Navies.

title: Battle of Eylau
description: The Battle of Eylau (also known as the Battle of Preussisch-Eylau) was a bloody and strategically inconclusive battle on 7 and 8 February [O.S. 26 and 27 Jan.] 1807 between Napoleon's Grande Armée and the Imperial Russian Army under the command of General Levin August von Bennigsen

title: Battle of Friedland
description: Fought on June 14, 1807, between the French army under Napoleon Bonaparte and the Russian army under General Levin August von Bennigsen

title: Battle of Berezina
description: Battle between Napoleon's Grande Armée and the Imperial Russian Army under Field Marshal Wittgenstein and Admiral Chichagov. Napoleon was retreating toward Poland in chaos after the aborted occupation of Moscow and trying to cross the Berezina River at Borisov.

title: Battle of Ligny
description: Fought on 16 June 1815French troops of the Armée du Nord under the command of Napoleon I defeated part of a Prussian army under Field Marshal Blücher.

title: Battle of Quatre Bras
description: Fought on 16 June 1815, as a preliminary engagement to the decisive Battle of Waterloo that occurred two days later.

title: Battle of Pultusk
description: Took place on December 26th 1806 Despite their strong numerical superiority and artillery, the Russians under the lead of General of Cavalry Levin von Benningsen underwent heavy French attacks by Marshal Jean Lannes.

title: Battle of Golymin
description: Fought on the same day as Pultusk, December 26th, this was another intense winter engagement between French and Russian forces in Poland.

title: Battle of Guttstadt-Deppen
description: A violent engagement in the Polish/Prussian region during the prelude to the final campaign of the Fourth Coalition.

title: Battle of Heilsberg
description: Took place on 10 June 1807. Napoleon confronted Russian general Bennigsen and his army. The battle was a bloody engagement that lasted for several hours, with heavy casualties on both sides.

title: Siege of Danzig
description: The Siege of Danzig (now Gdańsk, Poland) by French forces under Marshal Lefebvre, acting as the corps commander of the Grande Armée, during the War of the Fourth Coalition.

title: Battle of Ostrolenka
description: Fought on 16 February 1807 between a French force under General of Division Anne Jean Marie René Savary and a Russian force under Lieutenant General Ivan Essen.

title: Battle of Znaim
description: Following defeat at the Battle of Wagram, Archduke Charles retreated north into Bohemia hoping to regroup his battered forces. The French army had also suffered in the battle and did not give immediate pursuit. But two days after the battle, Napoleon ordered his troops north intending to defeat the Austrians once and for all.

title: Walcheren Campaign
description: The Walcheren Campaign was an unsuccessful British expedition to the Kingdom of Holland in 1809 intended to open another front in the Austrian Empire's struggle with France during the War of the Fifth Coalition.

title: Attack of Flushing
description: The British had captured Flushing on the day of his arrival to the war zone after a ferocious bombardment, and the surrounding towns on August 15th.

title: Battle of Ebelsberg
description: A bloody French assault to take a key bridge over the Traun River, clearing the way to Vienna.

title: Battle of Paris
description: Allied forces  including Prussian, Russian, and Austrian troops, marched into Paris, effectively ending French domination in Europe.

title: Battle of the Côa
description: Took place on 24 July 1810, on the banks of the Côa River, in the borderlands between Portugal and Spain. It was fought between a French division under Marshal Auguste de Marmont and a British division under General Robert Craufurd.

title: Landing at Vedbæk
description: British troops landed at Vedbæk on August 16th 1807 and began besieging Copenhagen.

title: Battle of Køge
description: Fought on 29 August 1807 between British troops besieging Copenhagen and Danish militia raised on Sjælland.

title: Battle of Classens Have
description: Fought on August 31st 1807 between British troops and Danish volunteers.

title: Embargo against Franceis declared.
description: England declares an embargo against France and its allies, including Denmark-Norway.

title: Napoleon's first abdication
description: Napoleon abdicates the French throne in favor of his son, who is not accepted by the Allies occupying Paris.

title: Signing of the Treaty of Fontainebleau
description: Signed between Napoleon Bonaparte and the Allies, ending the War of the Sixth Coalition.

title: Treaty of Saint Petersburg
description: England and Russia signed the Treaty of Saint Petersburg, sealing an alliance against France

title: Departure of the expedition to Egypt.
description: Aboard a fleet of 55 warships and 280 transport vessels, Napoleon sailed to Egypt.

title: Battle of Polotsk
description: It tool place from October 18th to the 20th during Napoleon's invasion of Russia.In this encounter the Russians under General Peter Wittgenstein attacked and defeated a Franco-Bavarian force under Laurent Gouvion Saint-Cyr.

title: Battle of Blanc-Nez and Gris-Nez
description: Fought on 18 July 1805 between a combined flotilla of the Batavian Navy and French Imperial Navy under Carel Hendrik Ver Huell and a Royal Navy fleet under Lord Keith during the War of the Third Coalition.

title: Invasion of Surinam
description: The invasion of Surinam, on April 25th 1804, was a British military campaign which resulted in the capture and occupation of the Dutch colony of Surinam in 1804.

title: Battle of Diamond Rock
description: Took place between 31 May and 2 June 1805 during the Napoleonic Wars, when a Franco-Spanish force dispatched under Captain Julien Cosmao was able to retake Diamond Rock, on the approach to Fort-de-France, from the British forces that had occupied it over a year before.

title: Battle of Cape Finisterre
description: On July 22nd 1805, the British fleet under Admiral Robert Calder fought an indecisive Naval battle against the combined Franco-Spanish fleet which was returning from the West Indies.

title: Battle of Donauwörth
description: This was the first engagement of Napoleon's 1805 Austrian campaign. French forces under marshals Joachim Murat and Nicolas Soult beat an Austrian army corps under Kienmayer on October 7th 1805 at Donauwörth and crossed the Danube.

title: Battle of Wertingen
description: On October 8th 1805, French forces led by Marshals Murat and Jean Lannes attacked a small Austrian corps commanded by Feldmarschall-Leutnant Franz Xaver von Auffenberg.

title: Battle of Günzburg
description: On October 9th 1805, Marshall Ney ordered a French division to seize a crossing over the Danube River at Günzburg in the face of a Habsburg Austrian army led by Feldmarschall-Leutnant Karl Mack von Lieberich.

title: Battle of Haslach-Jungingen
description: Also known as the Battle of Albeck, fought on October 11th 1805 at Ulm-Jungingen north of Ulm at the Danube between French and Austrian forces, was part of the Ulm campaign

title: Battle of Memmingen
description: It occurred on 14 October that year and culminated in the surrender of Karl Spangen to Jean-de-Dieu Soult's 4th Army Corps.

title: Battle of Elchingen
description: Fought on 14th October 1805, French forces under Michel Ney routed an Austrian corps led by Johann Sigismund Riesch.

title: Battle of Verona
description: Fought on October 18th 1805 between the French Army of Italy under the command of André Masséna and an Austrian army led by Archduke Charles.

title: Battle of Caldiero
description: Took place on October 30th 1805, pitting the French Armée d'Italie under Marshal André Masséna against an Austrian army under the command of Archduke Charles.

title: Battle of Mehrnbach
description: Took place on October 30th 1805, shortly after the surrender of the Austrian troops in Ulm, from where the road to Vienna lay open to the Grande Armée.

title: Battle of Wischau
description: Occurred on November 28th 1805 between the Russian and French armies. The conflict resulted in a minor Russian success.

title: Battle of Saltanovka
description: Also known as the Battle of Mohilew, took place on 23 July 1812 during the early stages of the 1812 French invasion of Russia.

title: Battle of Klyastitsy
description: Also called the Battle of Yakubovo or the Battle of Oboyarshchina, was a series of military engagements that took place on 30 July [O.S. 18 July] 1812 – 1 August 1812.

title: Battle of Aboukir
description: Fought on July 25th 1799 between the French Army of the Orient under Napoleon Bonaparte and an Ottoman army under Mustafa Pasha.

title: Battle of Abensberg
description: Took place on April 20th 1809 between a Franco-German force under the command of Emperor Napoleon I of France and a reinforced Austrian corps led by Feldmarschall-Leutnant Archduke Louis of Austria.

title: Battle of Landshut
description: Took place on April 21st 1809 between the French, Württembergers (VIII Corps) and Bavarians (VII Corps) under Napoleon, and the Austrians under the General Johann von Hiller.

title: Battle of Neumarkt-Sankt Veit
description: On April 24th 1809 saw a Franco-Bavarian force led by Marshal Jean-Baptiste Bessières face an Austrian Empire army commanded by Johann von Hiller.

title: Battle of Radzymin
description: Took place on 25 April 1809, during the brief Polish–Austrian War. The battle occurred at Radzymin, some 20 kilometres (12 mi) north-east of Warsaw.

title: Battle of Gospic
description: On May 21st 1809, Marmont located Rebrovich's forces deployed behind the Lika River near Gospić.

title: Battle of La Piave
description: Fought on May 8th 1809 between the Franco-Italian army under the command of Eugène de Beauharnais and an Austrian army led by Archduke John of Austria.

title: Battle of Wörgl
description: Fought on May 13th 1809, when a Bavarian force under French Marshal François Joseph Lefebvre attacked an Austrian Empire detachment commanded by Johann Gabriel Chasteler de Courcelles.

title: Battle of Tarvis
description: The two day battle took place from May 16th to May 17th 1809.

title: Battle of Malborghetto
description: The fortress was stormed by French troops on May 15th to May 17th 1809.

title: Battle of Linz-Urfahr
description: The Battle of Linz-Urfahr was fought on May 17th 1809, where the Austrian Empire Army fought against troops from two of Emperor Napoleon's allies, the Kingdom of Württemberg and the Kingdom of Saxony.

title: Battle of Sankt Michael
description: On May 25th 1809, Paul Grenier's French corps crushed Franz Jellacic's Austrian division at Sankt Michael in Obersteiermark, Austria.

title: Battle of the Basque Roads
description: The battle lasted from April 11th to the 24th 1809 and was a major Naval battle of the Napoleonic Wars, fought in the narrow Basque Roads at the mouth of the Charente River on the Biscay coast of France.

title: Battle of Raab
description: It was fought on June 14th 1809 between Franco-Italian forces and Habsburg forces. Most of the Habsburg army consisted of poorly trained Landwehr and militia.

title: Battle of Graz
description: It was fought on June 14th 1809 between Franco-Italian forces and Habsburg forces. Most of the Habsburg army consisted of poorly trained Landwehr and militia.

title: Combat of Korneuburg
description: It was a relatively minor rearguard action fought by Austrian VI Korps of the Kaiserlich-königliche Hauptarmee under Johann von Klenau against elements of the French IV Corps of the Armée d'Allemagne, under the command of Claude Legrand.

title: Masséna created Duke of Rivoli
description: On April 24, 1808, André Masséna was elevated to the rank of Duke of Rivoli by Napoleon Bonaparte for his role in crushing the Austrian forces in Italy.

title: Battle of Gefrees
description: Fought on July 8th 1809 during the War of the Fifth Coalition between a joint force of Austrians and Brunswickers under the command of General Kienmayer and a French force under the command of General Junot.

title: Battle of Hollabrunn
description: Was a rearguard action fought on July 9th 1809 by Austrian VI Korps of the Kaiserlich-königliche Hauptarmee under Johann von Klenau against elements of the French IV Corps of the Armée d'Allemagne, under the command of André Masséna

title: Battle of Möckern
description: Was a series of heavy clashes on April 5th 1813 between allied Prusso-Russian troops under Russian commander Peter Wittgenstein and Napoleonic French forces under Eugène de Beauharnais.

title: Battle of Luneburg
description: On April 2nd 1813, Allied Russians and Prussians were victorious over a French and Saxon division.

title: Battle of Luckau
description: Prussian and Russian forces under General Friedrich Wilhelm von Bülow defeated part of a French-Allied corps under Marshal Nicolas Oudinot on June 4th 1813.

title: Battle of Lützen
description: Prussian and Russian forces under General Friedrich Wilhelm von Bülow defeated part of a French-Allied corps under Marshal Nicolas Oudinot on May 2nd 1813.

title: Battle of Haynau
description: Fought on May 26th 1813, between Prussian cavalry under the command of General Gebhard Leberecht von Blücher and a French infantry division under the command of General Nicolas Joseph Maison

title: Battle of Grobbeeren
description: Occurred on August 23rd 1813 in neighboring Blankenfelde and Sputendorf between the Prussian III Corps under Friedrich von Bülow and the Franco-Saxon VII Corps under Jean Reynier.

title: Battle of the Katzbach
description: Fought on August 26th 1813, was a major battle of the Napoleonic Wars between the forces of the First French Empire under Marshal MacDonald and a Russo-Prussian army of the Sixth Coalition under Prussian Marshal Graf von Blücher.

title: Battle of Dresden
description: Fought on August 26th - 27th 1813, was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars. The battle took place around the city of Dresden in modern-day Germany, then the capital of the Kingdom of Saxony.

title: Battle of Hagelberg
description: Fought on August 27th 1813, was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars. The battle took place around the village of Hagelberg in modern-day Germany, then the capital of the Kingdom of Saxony.

title: Battle of Kulm
description: Fought on August 29th - 30th 1813, was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars. The battle took place around the village of Kulm in modern-day Czech Republic, then the capital of the Kingdom of Bohemia.

title: Battle of Dennewitz
description: Fought on September 6th 1813, was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars. The battle took place around the village of Dennewitz in modern-day Germany, then the capital of the Kingdom of Saxony.

title: Battle of Wartenburg
description: Took place on October 3rd 1813 between the French IV Corps commanded by General Henri Gatien Bertrand and the Allied Army of Silesia, principally the I Corps of General Ludwig von Yorck with the personal participation of the army commander, Gebhard L. von Blücher.

title: Appointment of the Commander of the Army of Portugal
description: Marshal André Masséna was appointed to command the Army of Portugal on April 17, 1810.

title: Battle of Sacile
description: Also known as the Battle of Fontana Fredda, saw an Austrian army commanded by Archduke John of Austria defeat a Franco-Italian army led by Eugène de Beauharnais and force it to retreat on April 16th 1809.

title: Batalla de la Poza de Sta Isabel
description: Also known as the capture of the Rosily squadron, took place on June 14th 1808 in Cádiz following the Dos de Mayo Uprising against French troops in Madrid.

title: Action of 4 April 1808
description: The Royal Navy warships HMS Alceste, HMS Mercury and HMS Grasshopper intercepted and defeated a large Spanish convoy protected by twenty gunboats and a train of batteries close to shore.

title: Action of 2 June 1807
description: During the British invasions of the River Plate between a Royal Navy schooner and two Spanish small vessels, a privateer sloop and naval felucca, at the mouth of the Paraná Guazú.

title: Evacuation of La Romana's division
description: On August 10th 1808 the Spaniard troops commanded by the Marquis of La Romana defected from the armies of the First French Empire.

title: Battle of Cape Santa Maria
description: Took place on October 5th 1804. In this confrontation, a British squadron under the command of Commodore Graham Moore attacked and defeated a Spanish squadron led by Brigadier Don José de Bustamante.

title: Action of 23 August 1806
description: In the engagement on August 23 1806, the Spanish frigate Pomona was captured by the British frigates HMS Anson and HMS Arethusa under the commands of Captain Charles Lydiard and Charles Brisbane

title: Battle of Cape Ortegal
description: On November 4th 1805 took place the final action of the Trafalgar campaign, and was fought between a squadron of the Royal Navy and a remnant of the fleet that had been defeated earlier at the Battle of Trafalgar.

title: Battle of Alvøen
description: On May 16th 1808 the British frigate HMS Tartar and five Norwegian gunboats off Bergen clashed as part of the Gunboat War.

title: Battle of Anholt
description: Fought on March 27th 1811, during the Gunboat War, it was an attempt by the Dano-Norwegians to recapture Anholt, a small Danish island off the coast of Jutland which the British had captured in 1809.

title: Battle of Copenhagen
description: Also known as the Bombardment of Copenhagen, it occurred between August 16th and September 7th 1807. British forces bombarded the Danish capital in order to capture or destroy the Royal Dano-Norwegian Navy.

title: Battle of Grand Port
description: Was fought from August 20th to the 27th 1810 between the British and French navies over the harbour of Grand Port, Isle de France, as part of the Mauritius campaign.

title: Invasion of Isle Bonaparte
description: The British invasion of the French island of Réunion, then known as Isle Bonaparte, was a successful operation by the British Royal Navy and Army against the French garrison. The island was captured on July 8th 1810.

title: Raid on Griessie
description: The British invasion of the French island of Réunion, then known as Isle Bonaparte, was a successful operation by the British Royal Navy and Army against the French garrison. The island was captured on July 8th 1810.

title: Battle of Jobourg
description: It was a minor naval engagement on March 26th to 27th 1814 between the British and French navies during the last weeks of the War of the Sixth Coalition.

title: Battle of Lissa
description: Also known as the Battle of Vis, was fought between a British frigate squadron and a much larger squadron of French and Italian frigates and smaller vessels on Wednesday, March 13th 1811 during the Adriatic campaign.

title: Battle of Zealand Point
description: It was fought off Zealand on March 22nd 1808 during the Gunboat War. Three ships of the line and one frigate of the Royal Navy intercepted and destroyed a Danish ship of the line.

title: Battle of Tamatave
description: It was fought off Tamatave in the Indian Ocean between British and French frigate squadrons during the Napoleonic Wars. The action was the final engagement of the Mauritius campaign of 1809/1811.

title: Battle of Les Sables-d'Olonne
description: It was a minor naval battle fought on February 23rd 1809 off the town of Les Sables-d'Olonne on the Biscay Coast of France between a French Navy squadron of three frigates and a larger British squadron of ships of the line.

title: Battle of the Nile (Aboukir Bay)
description: Also known as the Battle of Aboukir Bay, was fought between the Royal Navy and the French Navy at Aboukir Bay in Egypt between August 1st and 3rd 1798. It was the climax of the Mediterranean campaign of 1798.

title: Battle of Brienne
description: On January 29th 1814,an Imperial French army led by Emperor Napoleon attack Prussian and Russian forces commanded by Prussian Field Marshal Gebhard Leberecht von Blücher.

title: Battle of Castiglione
description: The French Army of Italy under General Napoleon Bonaparte attack an army of the Habsburg monarchy led by Feldmarschall Dagobert Sigmund von Wurmser on August 5th 1796.

title: Siege of Wittenberg ends
description: By 1813, the fortress had become an important assembly point and supply depot for the French fighting in eastern Germany. On January 15th 1814, the fortress was surrendered to the allied forces.

title: Combat of Hoyerswerda
description: It was a French victory on May 23rd 1813, which encouraged Marshal Oudinot to advance towards Berlin, after a Prussian attack on his positions was repulsed.

title: Battle of Mondovì
description: It was fought on April 21st 1796 between the French army of Napoleon Bonaparte and the army of the Kingdom of Sardinia-Piedmont led by Michelangelo Alessandro Colli-Marchi.

title: Battle of Dego
description: The Second Battle of Dego was fought on April 14th and 15th 1796 during the French Revolutionary Wars between French forces and Austro-Sardinian forces.

title: Battle of Halle (1806)
description: On October 17th 1806, a French corps led by Jean-Baptiste Bernadotte fought the Prussian Reserve under Eugene Frederick Henry, Duke of Württemberg.

title: Battle of Halle (1813)
description: The Battle of Halle was a spring campaign battle of the German War of Liberation, which took place on May 2nd 1813, at Halle on the Saale River in Germany.

title: Battle of Wavre
description: It was fought on June 18th and 19th 1815 and was the final major military action of the Hundred Days campaign and the Napoleonic Wars.

title: Action of Namur
description: The Prussians took the town of Namur after a heavy fight.

title: Battle of Thouars
description: This battle of Thouars took place on June 20th 1815 during the War in the Vendée of 1815; it was won by the army of the First Empire.

title: Battle of Villers-Cotterêts
description: Prussian surprise night attack by General Pirch II on June 28th 1815.

title: Battle of Ostrovno
description: It took place on July 25th 1812, between French forces under the command of King of Naples Joachim Murat and Russian forces under General Ostermann-Tolstoy and ended with the Russian forces retreating from the battlefield.

title: Battle of Vitebsk
description: Took place on July 26th and 27th 1812 and ended with the Russian forces making a strategic retreat from the battlefield.

title: Battle of Shevardino
description: Took place on September 5th [O.S. August 24th] 1812 between French and Russian troops, with a victory of the French army.

title: Battle of Krasnoi
description: It unfolded from November 15th to the 18th 1812 marking a critical episode in Napoleon's arduous retreat from Moscow.

title: Battle of Vyazma
description: It unfolded from November 15th to the 18th 1812 marking a critical episode in Napoleon's arduous retreat from Moscow.

title: Battle of Gorodechno
description: It took place on August 12th 1812 between an army of the Austrian and Saxon allies of Napoleonic France under the overall command of the Prince of Schwarzenberg and Russian troops under Alexander Tormasov at Gorodechno.

title: Battle of Valutino
description: Also called the battle of Lubino, took place on August 19th 1812 near Valutino and Lubino, between a corps of French and allied troops led by Marshal Ney, about 35,000 strong, and a strong rear-guard of General Barclay de Tolly's Russian army of about 25,000.

title: Battle of Dahlenkirchen
description: It took place on August 21st 1812, between French and allied troops, with a victory of the Russian army of about 6,000 against the French about 1,500 strong.

title: Battle of Mesothen
description: It took place from September 26th to October 1st 1812, between the Russian Corps of Finland and the French-allied Prussian Auxiliary Corps.

title: Battle of Tarutino
description: Russian troops under the general command of Bennigsen attacked and defeated French troops under Murat's command on October 18th 1812.

title: Battle of Chashniki
description: Fought on October 31st 1812, between Russian forces under General Wittgenstein, and the French Army, commanded by Marshal Victor.

title: Battle of La Rothière
description: February 1st 1814 saw the Coalition forces of the Austrian Empire, Russian Empire, Kingdom of Prussia, Kingdom of Bavaria, and Kingdom of Württemberg attack a French army led by Emperor Napoleon.

title: Combat of Lesmont
description: A Coalition force of Russians and Bavarians under generals Eugen of Wurtemberg and Carl Philipp von Wrede was defeated on February 2nd 1814 by a French force under general Joseph Lagrange.

title: Battle of Montmirail
description: It was fought on February 11th 1814 between a French force led by Emperor Napoleon and two Allied corps commanded by Fabian Wilhelm von Osten-Sacken and Ludwig Yorck von Wartenburg.

title: Battle of Montereau
description: An Imperial French army led by Emperor Napoleon fought a corps of Austrians and Württembergers commanded by Crown Prince Frederick William of Württemberg.

title: Battle of Lippa
description: It was an engagement which took place on September 7th 1813 in what is now Croatia where the Austrians defeated the Franco-Italians.

title: Battle of Arnhem
description: Also known as the storming of Arnhem, saw Friedrich Wilhelm Freiherr von Bülow's Prussian corps fight an Imperial French division under Henri François Marie Charpentier on November 30th 1813

title: Battle of Mormant
description: Emperor Napoleon I and a division of Russians under Count Peter Petrovich Pahlen clashed near the town of Mormant, some 50 km (31 mi) southeast of Paris on February 17th 1814.

title: Battle of Bayonne
description: It was the last major battle of the Peninsular War, ensued when the French garrison of Bayonne led by General of Division Pierre Thouvenot launched a sortie against a besieging force of British, Portuguese, and Spanish troops on April 14th 1814.

title: Battle of Nive
description: It was fought towards the end of the Peninsular War from December 9th to December 13th 1813. Arthur Wellesley, Marquess of Wellington's Anglo-Portuguese and Spanish army defeated Marshal Nicolas Soult's French army.

title: Battle of Czarnowo
description: At 4pm on December 23rd 1806 French troops under the eye of Emperor Napoleon I launched an evening assault crossing of the Wkra River against Lieutenant General Alexander Ivanovich Ostermann-Tolstoy's defending Russian Empire forces.

title: Battle of Laon
description: On March 9th 1814 Blücher's Prusso-Russian force, occupying a tough defensive position at the town of Laon, repelled attacks by and successfully counterattacked French Emperor Napoleon and his subordinate corps commander, Marshal Marmont.

title: Battle of Fère-Champenoise
description: It was fought on march 25th 1814 between two Imperial French corps led by Marshals Auguste de Marmont and Édouard Mortier, duc de Trévise and a larger Coalition force composed of cavalry from the Austrian Empire, Kingdom of Prussia, Kingdom of Württemberg, and Russian Empire.

title: Battle of Craonne
description: This battle between an Imperial French army under Emperor Napoleon I opposing a combined army of Imperial Russians and Prussians led by Prussian Field Marshal Gebhard Leberecht von Blücher fought on March 7th 1814

title: 2nd Battle of Bar-sur-Aube
description: It was fought on February 27th 1814 between a French army led by Marshal Nicolas Oudinot and a Coalition army of Austrians, Bavarians, Russians, and Württembergers led by Austrian Field Marshal Karl von Schwarzenberg.

title: 1st Battle of Bar-sur-Aube
description: It was fought on January 24th 1814 between Marshal Édouard Mortier, duc de Trévise's corps of French Imperial Guards defended against an Austrians corps under Ignaz Gyulai and a Württemberger corps led by Crown Prince Frederick William of Württemberg.

title: Battle of Nivelle
description: It took place in front of the river Nivelle with a 30 km front (20 miles) near the end of the Peninsular War.

title: Battle of Tolentino
description: was fought from May 2nd to May 3rd 1815 and it was the decisive battle in the Neapolitan War.

title: Battle of the Mincio River
description: Fought on February 8th 1814 on the same ground as Napoleon's victory at the Battle of Borghetto in 1796 resulted in an inconclusive engagement between the French under Eugène de Beauharnais and the Austrians under Field Marshal Heinrich von Bellegarde.

title: Battle of Nogent-sur-Seine
description: On February 11th 1814, Victor's troops at Nogent held out admirably against those of Wittgenstein and Wrede, but as Wrede troops corssed the Sena river, the French abandonned Nogent to the Russians.

title: Battle of Gilly
description: The Prussian commanded by General Pirch on June 15th 1815, retreat had scarcely begun when his retreating columns were vigorously assaulted by four Squadrons de Service of the Imperial Guard commanded by General Letort.

title: Battle of Heliopolis
description: French forces under General Kléber defeated the Ottoman army led by Nassif Pasha on March 20th 1800.

title: Battle of Alexandria
description: Also known as the Battle of Canope/Canopus[4]) was fought on March 21st 1801, where the British army and naval forces repelled an assault by the French army.

title: Siege of El Arish
description: The Siege of El Arish was a siege of the Ottoman fortress of El Arish by the French army, which took place from February 8th to 20th 1801 during the French campaign in Egypt.

title: Battle of Mandora
description: It was a minor battle fought on March 13th 1801 between French forces under François Lanusse and the British expeditionary corps under Ralph Abercromby.

title: Siege of Acre
description: It was an unsuccessful French two month siege of the Ottoman city started on March 20th 1799 and was the turning point of Napoleon's invasion of Egypt and Syria

title: Battle of Lübeck
description: It was fought on November 6th 1806 between French troops under Marshals Murat, Bernadotte, and Soult and Prussian troops under Blücher.

title: Capture of Alexandria
description: On July 2nd 1798, the French army landed and took the city of Alexandria from the hands of the Janissaries.

title: Battle of Shubra Khit
description: Also known as Shubra Khit,was the second major engagement of the French invasion of Egypt and Syria and took place on July 13th 1798.

title: Battle of Sediman
description: Also known as Battle of El Lahun,  took place on October 7, 1798, during the French invasion of Egypt. French troops under General Louis Desaix defeated the Mamluk forces led by Murad Bey, securing control over Upper Egypt.

title: Siege of Jaffa
description: The French laid siege to the city of Jaffa, which was under Ottoman control. It was fought from March 3rd to the 7th 1799, when French forces managed to capture the city.

title: Battle of Mohrungen
description: On January 25th 1807, most of a First French Empire corps under the leadership of Marshal Jean-Baptiste Bernadotte fought a strong Russian Empire advance guard led by Major General Yevgeni Ivanovich Markov.

title: Battle of Allenstein
description: Also known as the Battle of Jonkowo and the Battle of Bergfriede. The battle on February 3rd 1807 resulted in a French field victory and allowed for a successful pursuit of the Russian army.

title: Battle of Dürenstein
description: Also known as he Battle of Krems. On November 11th 1805 a combined force of Russian and Austrian troops trapped a French division commanded by Théodore Maxime Gazan.

title: Battle of Sabugal
description: It was a meeting engagement of the Peninsular War which took place on April 3rd 1811 between Anglo-Portuguese forces under Arthur Wellesley (later the Duke of Wellington) and French troops under the command of Marshal André Masséna.

title: Battle of Bussaco
description: It was fought on September 27th 1810 during the Peninsular War in the Portuguese mountain range of Serra do Buçaco, resulting in the defeat of French forces by Lord Wellington's Anglo-Portuguese Army.

title: French invasion of Malta
description: The French First Republic led by Napoleon Bonaparte successfully invaded the islands of Malta and Gozo, then ruled by the Knights Hospitaller, in June 10th 1798 as part of the Mediterranean campaign of the French Revolutionary Wars.

title: Naval Battle of Sandöström
description: The Swedes, whose fleet controlled the open sea, attempted to remove the Russian reinforcements.

title: Naval Action of 5 October 1804
description: a British squadron under the command of Commodore Graham Moore attacked and defeated a Spanish squadron led by Brigadier Don José de Bustamante.

title: Naval Battle of Palva Sound
description: The Battle of Palva Sund was fought between Sweden and Russia on September 18th 1808.

title: Battlefield of Gamonal
description: On November 11th 1808, a French army under Soult overwhelmed the outnumbered Spanish troops under General Belvedere, opening central Spain, including Madrid, to invasion.

title: Action of 25 September 1806
description: A French squadron comprising five frigates and two corvettes, sailing to the French West Indies with supplies and reinforcements, was intercepted by a British squadron of six ships of the line.

title: Napoleon enters in Milan
description: Napoleon enters in Milan, capital of the Duchy of Milan, and the city is given in his hands.

title: Napoleon crowned Kinf of Italy
description: Napoleon is crowned King of Italy at the Duomo in Milan.

title: Louisiana Purchase
description: The Louisiana Purchase treaty is signed. France sells the territory to the United States for $15 million to fund European wars.

title: Napoleon's arrival on Elba
description: Napoleon arrives at the Island of Elba to begin his first exile following his abdication at Fontainebleau.

title: Battle of Lodi
description: Battle of Lodi, where Napoleon Bonaparte won a decisive victory against the Austrians in 1796.

title: Napoleon captures Vienna
description: Napoleon captures Vienna fo r the second time during the War of the Fifth Coalition.

title: Napoleon crosses the Alps
description: Napoleon begins his famous Crossing of the Alps via the Great St Bernard Pass to surprise Austrian forces in Italy.

title: Napoleon enters Milan
description: Napoleon enters Milan in triumph after the victory at Lodi, establishing the Transpadane Republic.

title: Annexation of Papal States
description: Napoleon decrees the annexation of the Papal States into the French Empire, leading to his excommunication by Pope Pius VII.

title: Napoleon becomes Emperor of the French
description: The Senate proclaims Napoleon Emperor of the French, officially transitioning the Consulate into the First French Empire.

title: Battle of Borghetto
description: On May 30th 1796, a French army led by General Napoleon Bonaparte forced a crossing of the Mincio River in the face of opposition from an Austrian army commanded by Feldzeugmeister Johann Peter Beaulieu.

title: Action of 22 May 1812
description: A small French squadron comprising the French frigates Ariane and Andromaque, and the brig Mameluck returning from a commerce raiding campaign in the Atlantic, met the 74-gun HMS Northumberland while trying the slip to Lorient through the British blockade.
location: off the coast of Penmarch, France

title: Battle of Lyngør
description: Fought between the British and Dano-Norwegian navies as part of the Gunboat War.

title: Battle of Blaauwberg
description: Also known as the Battle of Cape Town, it was a successful British amphibious operation during the War of the Third Coalition which lasted from 8–18 January 1806 and resulted in the capture of the Dutch Cape Colony.

title: Battle of Grijó
description: Fought on May 10th and 11th 1809, iteended in victory for the Anglo-Portuguese Army commanded by Lieutenant-General Sir Arthur Wellesley (the future 1st Duke of Wellington) over the French army commanded by Marshal Nicolas Soult during the Second French invasion of Portugal.

title: Battle of Braga
description: The Battle of Braga or Battle of Póvoa de Lanhoso or Battle of Carvalho d'Este (March 20th 1809) saw an Imperial French corps led by Marshal Jean-de-Dieu Soult attack a Portuguese army commanded by Baron Eben

title: Battle of Évora
description: The Battle of Évora on July 29th 1808 saw an Imperial French division under Louis Henri Loison attack a combined Portuguese-Spanish force led by Francisco de Paula Leite de Sousa.

title: Battle of Redinha
description: It was a rearguard action which took place on March 12th 1811, during Masséna's retreat from Portugal as part of the Peninsular War, by a French division under Marshal Ney against a considerably larger Anglo-Portuguese force under Wellington.

title: Battle of El Bodon
description: It was a rearguard action fought on September 25th 1811 by elements of the Anglo-Portuguese army against waves of French cavalry, supported by a division of infantry of the French army.

title: Battle of Maloyaroslavets
description: Russian and French armies were evenly matched in the battle and suffered similar casualties, with a marginal edge going to the Franco-Italian army.

title: Fisrt Battle of Polotsk
description: Russian troops under the general command of Bennigsen, acting on instructions from Kutuzov and being part of his army, launched a surprise attack and defeated French troops under Murat's command.

title: Battle of Wolkowisk
description: Took place from November 14th to the 16th 1812. Austrian, Saxon and French soldiers under Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg defeated the Russian army under Fabian Gottlieb von der Osten-Sacken

title: Battle of Borizov
description: The Russian vanguard under Charles de Lambert eliminated the Franco-Polish force under Dombrowsky.

title: Battle of Swolna
description: Oudinot's French ran at the river Svolna into the Russians under d'Auvray.

title: Battle of Mir
description: Three Polish Lancers divisions battled against Russian cavalry, ending in the first major Russian victory in the war.

title: Napoleon enters in Milan
description: French troops led by Napoleon enter Milan in triumph during the Second Italian Campaign.

title: Truce of Pläswitz
description: The Truce or Armistice of Pläswitz was a nine-week armistice during the Napoleonic Wars, agreed between Napoleon I of France and the Allies.

title: First battle of El Bruc
description: French detachment of 3,800 soldiers under General of Brigade François Xavier de Schwarz was defeated by a force of 1,600 Spanish regulars and militia under Joan Sala i Ferrer and Antoni Franch i Estalella.

title: Second Battle of Dego
description: On June 14th 1808 a French detachment led by General of Division Joseph Chabran, succeeded only in putting to the torch several buildings in El Bruc after having been defeated and repelled by the Spanish forces led by Joan Baget.

title: Sir Thomas Picton birth
description: He was born in Haverfordwest, Pembrokeshire on (probably) August 24th 1758 and was the seventh of twelve children of Thomas Picton

title: Marshall Blücher birth
description: He was born in Rostock, Prussia on December 16th 1742, son of a retired army captain.

title: 'El Empecinado'birth
description: Born on September 2nd 1775 in Castrillo de Duero, Valladolid, he became a notorious guerilla leader and a hero of the Spanish War of Independence.

title: Battle of Nargö
description: The battle between the Russian cutter Opyt and the British frigate HMS Salsette took place near the northern coast of Estonia by the island of Naissaar (Nargö) in the Baltic Sea, and ended in a British victory.

title: Marshall Masséna birth
description: André Masséna was born on May 6th 1758 in Nice, France.

title: Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg birth
description: Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg was born on April 15th 1771 in Vienna, Austria. Some sources give the date of his birth as April 18th or 19th 1771.

title: Battle of Prezlau
description: The Battle of Prenzlau or Capitulation of Prenzlau on 28 October 1806 two divisions of French cavalry and some infantry led by Marshal Joachim Murat intercepted a retreating Prussian corps led by Frederick Louis, Prince of Hohenlohe-Ingelfingen.

title: Siege of Breslau
description: The siege of Breslau took place from December 7, 1806, until January 9, 1807, when the city capitulated to the French army.

title: Mikhail Kutuzov birth
description: Mikhail Kutuzov was born on September 16th, although 1745 is often cited, newly identified documents dated it to 1747. It is traditionally believed that he was born in St. Petersburg, although this was not confirmed when checking the city's parish records for 1745-1748. The place of birth is also called various settlements of the Pskov province (modern Pskov region).

title: Battle on Os'ma river
description: The battle took place on the Os'ma river, near the village of Semlevo, on August 27th 1812 when Murat's cavalry attacked the Russian rearguard. The Russians were able to hold off the French and prevent them from cutting off the retreating Russian army.

title: Battle of Molevo Boloto (Inkovo)
description: The battle took place on 8 August 1812, between the Cossack corps of General Platov and the 2nd Light Cavalry Division of General Sebastiani and ended as a minor Russian victory.

title: Pyotr Bagration birth
description: Pyotr Bagration was born on July 10th 1765 in Kizlyar, Dagestan, where his father served as a military officer.

title: Napoleon faces his final exile.
description: Napoleon Bonaparte was transferred from HMS Bellerophon to the 74-gun HMS Northumberland in Torbay, England. This occurred after his surrender following the Battle of Waterloo.

title: Napoleon departs to Saint Helena
description: Under the command of Rear Admiral Sir George Cockburn, the fleet departed England on August 8th 1815. During the voyage to his final exile on Saint Helena.

title: Napoleon arrives to Saint Helena
description: On October 15th 1815, after two month sailing the Atlantic Ocean, Napoleon arrived at Jamestown, Saint Helena, his final place of exile.

title: First day of the battle of Lonato
description: The French Army of Italy under General Napoleon Bonaparte and a corps-sized Austrian column led by Lieutenant General Peter Quasdanovich.

title: Second day of the battle of Lonato
description: A week of hard-fought actions that began on 29 July and ended on 4 August resulted in the retreat of Quasdanovich's badly mauled force.

title: Dissolution of the Holy Roman Empire
description: On August 6, 1806, Francis II abdicated and formally dissolved the Holy Roman Empire. This monumental event in the Napoleonic Wars was forced by Napoleon Bonaparte, who had recently established the Confederation of the Rhine, stripping the ancient empire of its German states and neutralizing Austrian power.

title: The Mobilization of Prussia
description: Prussia's decision to partially mobilize its forces on August 9 occurred due to growing outrage over Napoleon Bonaparte's expanding influence in Germany. King Frederick William III, alarmed by French activities in Hanover and the formation of the Confederation of the Rhine, began preparing for war.

title: Napoleon's response to the Prussian Mobilization
description: On August 25th 1806, Napoleon ordered Marshal Murat to march on Berlin with his cavalry. This was in response to the Prussian mobilization on August 9th, which was seen as a threat to French control of Germany.

title: The Antwerp Siege Preparations
description: During the War of the Fifth Coalition, the fortified port city of Antwerp began preparing for a siege. This defensive posture was a direct reaction to the Walcheren Campaign, where a large British expeditionary force had landed in the Netherlands to destroy French naval assets.

title: First Siege of Zaragoza ends
description: The First Siege of Zaragoza ended on August 13th 1808 when Spanish irregulars and citizens successfully repulsed the French troops, forcing them to retreat. This marked a major early setback for French forces in the Peninsular War.

title: Napoleon begins the return from Egypt
description: After leaving General Kléber in command of the French forces in Egypt, Napoleon set sail for France on August 23rd 1799. His return would prove pivotal for the future of his career.

title: British Landing in Portugal
description: British forces under Sir Arthur Wellesley landed in Portugal, signaling the first time a British army took the lead in a land war against Napoleon in Europe.

title: Siege of Retiro
description: British and Allied forces successfully stormed the outer defenses of the Retiro fortress in Madrid following the Battle of Salamanca.

title: Napoleon Bonaparte Birthday
description: Napoleon Bonaparte was born on August 15th 1769 in Ajaccio, Corsica. During the Napoleonic Wars, it was celebrated as the French national holiday.

title: Jérôme Bonaparte crowned King of Westphalia
description: Napoleon's youngest brother, Jérôme Bonaparte, was crowned King of Westphalia on December 8th 1807. He reigned until 1813, when the Confederation of the Rhine collapsed following Napoleon's defeat.

title: First Battle of Polotsk
description: took place on 17- 18 August 1812, Russian troops under the command of Peter Wittgenstein fought French and Bavarian troops led by Nicolas Oudinot near the city of Polotsk, halting Oudinot's advance toward Saint Petersburg once more as at the Battle of Klyastitsy. It was a strategic Russian success, but tactically the battle ended in a draw.

title: Adoption of the Constitution of Year III
description: The National Convention adopted the Constitution of Year III, establishing the French Directory. This shift in power paved the way for Napoleon Bonaparte's subsequent rise to prominence during the coup of 18 Brumaire.

title: Marshal Bernadotte Elected Crown Prince of Sweden
description: On August 24, 1810, the Swedish parliament (Riksdag) formally elected French Marshal Jean-Baptiste Bernadotte as the new crown prince to the Swedish throne.

title: Napoleon Changes the Grand Army's Focus to Austria
description: On August 9th 1809, Napoleon issued a series of orders that effectively shifted the Grand Army's main operational focus from Spain to Austria, setting the stage for the upcoming War of the Fifth Coalition.

title: Siege of Toulon
description: An Anglo-Spanish fleet arrived on August 28th 1793 transporting 13,000 British, Spanish, Neapolitan and Sardinian troops. As Toulon was strategically vital, being a major naval port which was then host to a third of the French Navy's ships of the line, the French Republican government quickly moved to recapture the city. Here is where Napoleon's military reputation begun.

title: Second day of the battle of Kulm
description: On the second day of the battle of Kulm, the Württembergers and Bavarians launched repeated heavy attacks against the Russian positions on the Nollendorf heights. Despite capturing several trenches, they were ultimately repulsed by the determined Russian defense under General Miloradovich.

title: Convention of Cintra
description: The Convention of Cintra (or Sintra) was an agreement signed on 30 August 1808, during the Peninsular War. By the agreement, the defeated French were allowed to evacuate their troops from Portugal without further conflict.

title: The Congress of Vienna Technically Convenes
description: The Congress of Vienna of 1814-1815 was a series of international diplomatic meetings to discuss and agree upon a possible new layout of the European political and constitutional order after the downfall of the French Emperor Napoleon Bonaparte.

title: The Russian Army Halts at Borodino
description: On September 3, 1812, the retreating Russian army halted and began digging into defensive positions near the village of Borodino. Over the next three days, they heavily fortified the landscape with massive earthworks.

title: Blücher Advances Across the Quéis River
description: On September 3, 1813, Blücher's Army of Silesia successfully forced a crossing of the Quéis River (Queis/Kwisa), driving the French rearguard under Marshal Jacques Macdonald further back toward the Elbe River and steadily tightening the noose around Napoleon's main forces in Saxony.

title: Britain Demands the Surrender of the Danish Fleet (Gunboat War)
description: During the War of the Second Coalition, Great Britain grew deeply concerned that Napoleon would seize Denmark's powerful navy to launch an invasion of the British Isles. British forces laid siege to Copenhagen, and on September 4, 1807, following consecutive days of heavy artillery and incendiary bombardment that devastated the city, the Danish government was forced to enter formal surrender negotiations.

title: Napoleon Enters Mozhaysk (Invasion of Russia)
description: On September 4, 1812, as Napoleon's Grande Armée relentlessly pushed toward Moscow, French vanguard forces entered the town of Mozhaysk, just a few miles west of the village of Borodino. This move triggered intense cavalry skirmishes as the Russian rearguard under General Konovnitsyn fought a desperate delaying action.

title: Prussia mobilizes the army
description: King Frederick William III of Prussia mobilized roughly 130,000 Prussian and 20,000 Saxon troops. Emboldened by promises of Russian and British support, the Prussian generals planned an aggressive offensive into central Germany.

title: Prussia Declared Ready to Attack (War of the Fourth Coalition)
description: Frustrated by Napoleon's expanding dominance, the King committed to military action against 'the disturber of the rest of the world'. On 6 September 1806, King Frederick William III of Prussia wrote a letter to Emperor Alexander I of Russia declaring his readiness to attack Napoleon.

title: Marshals Davout and Ney receive orders to join their Army Corps.
description: On September 19, 1806, Napoleon issued crucial directives for the Grande Armée, formally ordering Marshals Louis-Nicolas Davout and Michel Ney to immediately join their respective army corps as the empire mobilized for war against Prussia.

title: Napoleon enters Berlin
description: After defeating the Prussians at Jena-Auerstedt, Napoleon entered Berlin, the capital of Prussia, on October 27th 1806. This victory marked the end of the War of the Fourth Coalition and established French dominance over central Europe.

title: Napoleon sent a call to his German allies.
description: On 20 September 1806, Napoleon I issued a formal call for troops and support from his German allies in the recently established Confederation of the Rhine. This mobilization occurred in preparation for the upcoming War of the Fourth Coalition against Prussia.

title: The French Imperial Guard left Paris
description: On September 24th 1806, the French Imperial Guard left Paris by order of Napoleon Bonaparte. This move marked the logistical start of the Prussian Campaign (War of the Fourth Coalition).

title: Napoleon left Saint-Cloud for the Prussian Campaign
description: Alerted by Marshal Berthier about Prussian troop movements near the border, Napoleon immediately left for the front lines. He departed the Castle of Saint-Cloud with Empress Joséphine at 4:30 a.m., traveling east through the day and continuing his journey through the night after dining in Châlons.

title: French Senate Conscription Levy
description: Anticipating escalating conflicts on multiple fronts, particularly in Spain, the French Senate approved a massive special levy of 160,000 soldiers.

title: Russian Retreat from Borodino
description: After the bloody Battle of Borodino, Barclay de Tolly, commanding the Russian forces, executed a strategic retreat from the battlefield. This decision allowed the Russians to preserve their army intact, despite the tactical loss of the field itself.

title: Final Surrender of San Sebastián
description: Following the devastating bombardment of Monte Urgull the previous day, French Governor Louis Emmanuel Rey marched his remaining, battle-worn garrison out of the castle with full military honors.

title: Restoration of the Gregorian Calendar
description: Anticipating the outbreak of major hostilities in the War of the Third Coalition, Napoleon signed a Senatus Consultum officially abolishing the French Republican Calendar. The decree ordered the restoration of the traditional Gregorian calendar to align France with the administrative and logistical structures of continental Europe, taking effect on January 1, 1806.

title: The Treaty of Teplitz
description: During the German Campaign, the Sixth Coalition was formally solidified when Russia, Austria, and Prussia signed the Treaty of Töplitz (Teplitz). The signatories committed to placing 150,000 troops each in the field and bound themselves to make no separate peace with Napoleon until the French Empire was pushed back to its pre-war natural borders.

title: Naples Joins the Third Coalition
description: In a major diplomatic shift ahead of the Ulm and Austerlitz campaigns, the Kingdom of Naples and Sicily officially joined the Anglo-Austro-Russian Coalition. This move effectively opened up a dangerous southern front against French-aligned Italy, prompting a future decisive response from Napoleon.

title: Annexation of Piedmont
description: During the brief interlude of peace following the Treaty of Amiens, Napoleon consolidated French hegemony over northern Italy. Through a Senatus consultum, France officially annexed the region of Piedmont, dividing it directly into six new French administrative departments.

title: Kutuzov's Council of Fili
description: In a modest log cabin in the village of Fili, just outside Moscow, Russian General Mikhail Kutuzov convened a legendary council of war. Despite fierce opposition from several generals, Kutuzov made the agonizing strategic decision to abandon Moscow to Napoleon without a fight, famously declaring that losing Moscow did not mean losing Russia, whereas losing the army would mean losing both.

title: Establishment of the Regency Council
description: As Napoleon prepared to march the Grande Armée eastward into Germany for the Ulm Campaign, he signed an imperial decree in Paris forming a temporary Regency Council. This administrative body, spearheaded by Cambacérès and Joseph Bonaparte, was tasked with governing domestic French affairs while the Emperor was away on campaign.

title: Napoleon Enters Moscow
description: Expecting a delegation of city officials offering surrender, Napoleon Bonaparte rode into Moscow only to find it a virtual ghost town. The Russian army and roughly 95% of the civilian population had completely evacuated, leaving behind an empty prize.

title: The Advance on Krasnoy
description: Following the occupation of Moscow, French vanguard forces under Marshal Joachim Murat pushed outward to locate the retreating Russian army under Mikhail Kutuzov. On this day, initial clashes near the outskirts of Krasnoy signaled that the Russians were repositioning to cut off French supply lines.

title: British Naval Blockade Expansion
description: During the War of the Third Coalition, the British Royal Navy tightened its economic and military stranglehold on French-controlled ports along the English Channel. On this date, the Admiralty issued strict orders to step up the interception of Dutch and French merchant vessels to starve Napoleon of naval supplies.

title: The Raid on the Elbe
description: During the German Campaign, Allied light troops and Cossacks executed a daring raid across the Elbe River. They successfully intercepted a French logistical convoy, destroying military stores and further strangling Napoleon's overextended communication networks leading back to Dresden.

title: Siege of Burgos Escalates
description: In the Peninsular War, Arthur Wellesley (Wellington) intensified his siege of the French-held fortress of Burgos. On this day, Allied troops launched a fierce assault on the outer hornwork of San Miguel, suffering heavy casualties but securing a tactical foothold that escalated the brutal, month-long siege.

title: Creation of the Confederation of the Rhine
description: In one of Napoleon's most impactful political maneuvers, 16 German states signed the Treaty of the Confederation of the Rhine. This act officially severed their ties with the Holy Roman Empire and placed them under Napoleon's direct protection as a massive military buffer state. It effectively caused the collapse of the thousand-year-old Holy Roman Empire less than a month later.

title: Wellington Outmaneuvers Marmont
description: Leading up to the famous Battle of Salamanca in the Peninsular War, the Anglo-Portuguese army under Arthur Wellesley (Wellington) engaged in a high-stakes chess match of forced marches along the Guareña River. On this day, Wellington successfully shifted his lines, preventing French Marshal Auguste de Marmont from cutting off his supply routes to Portugal.

title: Signing of the Concordat of 1801
description: In Paris, Napoleon Bonaparte (then First Consul) and representatives of Pope Pius VII signed the Concordat. This historic agreement reconciled the French revolutionary state with the Catholic Church, restoring the Church's civil status while cementing Napoleon's political control over the selection of bishops and church property.

title: The Siege of Girona Intensifies
description: Following months of blockading the strategic Catalan city during the Peninsular War, French forces under General Laurent de Gouvion Saint-Cyr launched heavy artillery bombardments against the outer mountain forts of Girona. This day marked a brutal escalation in the siege, as Spanish defenders refused early French demands to capitulate.

title: The Trachenberg Plan Refinements
description: During the summer armistice of the German Campaign, Allied sovereigns and military commanders finalized the details of the crucial Trachenberg Plan. This overarching strategy dictated that Coalition armies would systematically avoid direct battle with Napoleon himself, choosing instead to retreat when he advanced and focus entirely on defeating his independent marshals.

title: Combat of Castrejón
description: Continuing their intense maneuvering before the Battle of Salamanca, French Marshal Auguste de Marmont attempted to surprise and cut off Wellington's Fourth and Light Divisions at Castrejón. A desperate rearguard action and a brilliant forced march allowed the Allied troops to escape the trap and reunite with the main army.

title: Battle of Grand Port Preparations
description: In the intense naval war for control of the Indian Ocean, a British squadron arrived off the coast of Isle de France (now Mauritius). The initial coastal skirmishes on this day set the stage for the famous Battle of Grand Port, which would become one of the Royal Navy's most significant tactical naval defeats of the war.

title: Battle of Bailén
description: The Battle of Bailén was fought during the Spanish War of Independence and marked the first open-field defeat in the history of Napoleon's army. It took place on July 19, 1808, near the town of Bailén in the province of Jaén. It pitted a French army of approximately 22,000 soldiers under General Dupont against a larger Spanish army (around 29,770) commanded by General Teodoro Reding, although the commander-in-chief of the so-called Army of Andalusia was General Castaños.

title: Surrender at Bailén
description: Following the fierce fighting that began the previous day, French General Pierre Dupont de l'Étang realized his imperial division was entirely surrounded and cut off. On July 20, he entered into formal negotiations that led to the complete surrender of over 17,000 French troops to the Spanish forces, a catastrophic blow to French military prestige in Europe.

title: The Siege of Mantua Escalation
description: During his first Italian campaign, Napoleon intensified the siege of the vital Austrian fortress city of Mantua. On this day, French batteries unleashed a massive, coordinated artillery bombardment against the city walls, attempting to force a quick surrender before Austrian relief columns could arrive.

title: The Evacuation of Segovia
description: Just four days after his brilliant victory at the Battle of Salamanca, Wellington's Allied army forced the French to abandon central Spain. On this day, French troops officially evacuated the historic city of Segovia, retreating toward Madrid and leaving behind vital military stores as French control in the region began to rapidly unravel.

title: Battle of Talavera Begins
description: In a major engagement of the Peninsular War, a combined Anglo-Spanish army under Wellington and General Gregorio de la Cuesta took up defensive positions at Talavera de la Reina to block a French army led by Marshal Victor and King Joseph Bonaparte. Evening French assaults on July 27 focused on a strategic hill (Medellín), sparking intense night fighting that set the stage for the main battle the following day.

title: The Russian Retreat to Smolensk
description: Following the three-day Battle of Vitebsk, Russian General Michael Andreas Barclay de Tolly successfully slipped away from Napoleon's enveloping maneuvers under the cover of night. By July 27, the Russian First Army was in full, orderly retreat toward Smolensk to unite with Bagration's forces, frustrating Napoleon’s attempts to force a decisive, early engagement.

title: The Battle of Talavera Fallout
description: In the immediate aftermath of the bloody battle, Arthur Wellesley (Wellington) received critical intelligence that a massive French army under Marshal Nicolas Soult was rapidly marching south to cut off his line of retreat into Portugal. On July 31, Wellington made the vital decision to abandon his advance on Madrid and begin a swift southwestward march to escape the trap.

title: Combat of Senno
description: During the French invasion of Russia, French forces under Marshal Victor clashed with the Russian rear-guard under General Peter Wittgenstein near Senno. This sharp tactical encounter was part of the heavy fighting along the northern flank as both sides attempted to secure the roads leading toward Vitebsk.

title: Battle of Tamames
description: The Battle of Tamames (October 18, 1809) was a Spanish victory over the French occupation troops near this town in Salamanca province, during the Spanish War of Independence. General Jean Gabriel Marchand, commanding the French IV Corps in Ney's absence, faced the so-called Army of the Left, commanded by the Duke of Parque.

title: Napoleon Lands in Egypt
description: Initiating his ambitious Mediterranean campaign, Napoleon Bonaparte arrived off the coast of Alexandria with a massive French expeditionary fleet. French troops immediately began landing on the beaches of Marabout under difficult conditions, preparing to capture Alexandria the following day and march into the Egyptian desert.
