title: The Decree of the Kingdom of Holland Annexation
description: Frustrated by his brother Louis Bonaparte's refusal to strictly enforce the Continental Blockade against British trade, Napoleon pressured him into abdicating the throne on this day. This political crisis led directly to France officially annexing the Kingdom of Holland into the French Empire just days later.

title: Crossing of the Danube / Battle of Wagram Prelude
description: Following his costly setback at Aspern-Essling, Napoleon executed a brilliant tactical operation under the cover of a violent thunderstorm. On the night of July 4, French forces successfully crossed the Danube River onto the Marchfeld, bypassing Austrian fortifications and setting the stage for the massive Battle of Wagram the next day.

title: The Siege of Mantua Inundations
description: During his first Italian campaign, Napoleon utilized the local geography to tighten his grip on the Austrian fortress of Mantua. On this date, French engineers opened the local sluice gates, flooding the surrounding plains to completely cut off the city's land communications and isolate the garrison.

title: The Battle of Wagram - Second Day
description: Following a massive tactical buildup, Napoleon fought the decisive second day of the Battle of Wagram against the Austrian army under Archduke Charles. Involving over 300,000 soldiers, the clash featured a legendary grand artillery battery and a massive infantry assault led by General MacDonald. The French victory broke the Fifth Coalition and forced Austria to sue for peace.

title: The Surrender of Alexandria
description: Just six days after landing on the Egyptian coast, Napoleon’s vanguard completed its lightning assault on the ancient city of Alexandria. On July 7, after crushing the local garrison, French administrative teams officially secured the port city, establishing it as the primary supply base for the deep march toward Cairo.

title: The Action at Santo Domingo
description: In the Caribbean theater of the Napoleonic Wars, a combined British-Spanish expeditionary force successfully compelled the isolated French garrison at Santo Domingo to surrender. This action effectively wiped out the last remnants of French imperial military presence on the island of Hispaniola.

title: Surrender of the Burgos Hornwork
description: During the bloody Siege of Burgos in the Peninsular War, Arthur Wellesley (Wellington) launched a fierce night assault. Allied troops successfully captured the strategic San Miguel hornwork, a vital outer fortification, though they suffered heavy casualties in the process.

title: French Consolidation of the Ionian Islands
description: Following the enforcement of the Treaties of Tilsit, French imperial troops officially finished occupying the strategic Ionian Islands (including Corfu). This move secured a vital naval stepping stone for Napoleon in the Adriatic Sea, aiming to project power toward the Ottoman Empire.

title: Siege of Burgos Mine Explosion
description: During the Peninsular War, Arthur Wellesley (Wellington) attempted to breach the formidable walls of the French-held fortress of Burgos. On this day, Allied engineers detonated a massive gunpowder mine beneath the fort's outer defenses; however, the subsequent infantry assault failed to secure a breakthrough due to fierce French resistance.

title: Siege of Burgos Escalation
description: Frustrated by earlier failed assaults in the Peninsular War, Arthur Wellesley (Wellington) ordered his engineers to dig a second, larger military mine under the formidable walls of the French-held fortress of Burgos. On this day, the frantic digging reached beneath the main French curtain wall, setting the stage for another bloody explosion and assault.

title: The Siege of Burgos Assault Preparations
description: In the Peninsular War, after completing the placement of their second massive gunpowder mine beneath the fortress walls, Arthur Wellesley's (Wellington) Anglo-Portuguese troops spent this day stockpiling ammunition and organizing storming parties, preparing for a decisive breach of the French defenses scheduled for the next morning.

title: The Skirmish at Borna
description: During the autumn German Campaign, a massive body of Allied Coalition cavalry—composed of Russian Cossacks and Prussian Hussars—clashed with French rear-guard units near Borna. This sharp encounter was part of the grand Allied encirclement strategy designed to cut off Napoleon's retreat paths toward France before the Battle of Leipzig.

title: The Second Mine Explosion at Burgos
description: During the brutal Siege of Burgos in the Peninsular War, Arthur Wellesley (Wellington) ordered the detonation of his second massive gunpowder mine beneath the fortress walls. The explosion successfully blew a massive breach in the outer wall, but the subsequent Anglo-Portuguese infantry assault was ultimately repulsed with heavy casualties by a fierce French counterattack.

title: The Battle of Jena-Auerstedt
description: In one of the most decisive military victories of the Napoleonic era, Napoleon executed a brilliant double-battle maneuver against the Prussian army. By attacking simultaneously at Jena and Auerstedt, French forces shattered the Prussian defenses in a single day, effectively ending Prussian resistance in the War of the Fourth Coalition and opening the path to Berlin.

title: The Treaty of San Ildefonso
description: In a secret diplomatic maneuver, France and Spain signed the Third Treaty of San Ildefonso. Spain agreed to return the massive territory of Louisiana in North America to France in exchange for territorial compensations in Tuscany for the Spanish royal family. This event set the stage for Napoleon's eventual sale of the territory to the United States three years later.

title: Blücher Crosses the Elbe
description: In a daring move during the German Campaign, Prussian General Gebhard Leberecht von Blücher concentrated his forces at Elster and began bridging operations to cross the Elbe River. This aggressive advance directly threatened Napoleon's northern flank, paving the way for the decisive Battle of Wartenburg two days later.

title: The French Senate Breaks with Austria
description: During the War of the Third Coalition, Napoleon officially announced a total diplomatic break with Austria before the French Senate. Seeking to bolster his ranks for the upcoming continental campaign, he pulled forward the conscription timeline, calling up 60,000 men from the class of 1806 early. This political escalation cleared the way for Napoleon to take command of the army the next day and order French troops across the Rhine, culminating in the Ulm Campaign.

title: The Siege of Saguntum Begins
description: French Marshal Louis Gabriel Suchet initiated the Siege of Saguntum on the eastern coast of Spain as part of the Peninsular War. Believing the province of Valencia would fall quickly, Suchet confronted a fiercely resilient Spanish garrison entrenched inside the ancient, rocky hilltop citadel. The castle defenders successfully repelled multiple French infantry assaults. This unexpected resistance dragged out the siege for over a month, significantly slowing down the French offensive in eastern Spain.

title: Napoleon Prepares the Prussian Campaign at Mainz
description: early in the morning to establish his operational headquarters. After parting with Empress Joséphine, Napoleon spent the day conducting high-level military interviews, notably with the famous military theorist General Antoine-Henri Jomini. This date marked the final logistical and intellectual orchestration of the Grande Armée right before it marched out to crush the Prussian army at Jena-Auerstedt.

title: Strategic Regrouping Before Leipzig
description: During the War of the Sixth Coalition, Napoleon recognized the growing threat of Allied armies closing in on his positions in Saxony. On October 2, he ordered Marshal Joachim Murat to Freiberg to take command of a 45,000-man strike force. Murat's explicit mission was to slow down and block the advance of the massive Allied Army of Bohemia under Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg, setting the tactical stage for the colossal Battle of Leipzig.

title: Napoleon Mobilizes the Ulm Campaign at Würzburg
description: During the War of the Third Coalition, Napoleon arrived at Würzburg to personally consolidate his forces. This date marked a pivotal logistical transition: Napoleon united his newly christened Grande Armée with German allied troops, including the Bavarian army. From this position, he finalized the brilliant strategic envelopment that would completely trap Austrian General Mack at Ulm just a few weeks later, altering the course of the continental war.

title: Herceg Novi was ceded to France
description: On 7 July 1807, Herceg Novi was ceded to Napoleon I Bonaparte's French Empire as per the Treaty of Tilsit. Official French rule over Herceg Novi began on 12 August 1807, when the Russians left the city. The city was part of Dalmatia until 14 October 1809, when it was annexed to the newly created Illyrian Provinces.

title: Moscow Peace Proposal
description: Stranded in a burning Moscow, Napoleon sent General Lauriston to deliver a peace offer to Russian Commander Kutuzov, who intentionally accepted the talks simply to stall for time.

title: The Crossing of the Danube
description: Elements of Napoleon's Grande Armée began crossing the Danube River at Donauwörth, successfully cutting off Austrian General Mack's line of retreat at Ulm.

title: Proclamation of the Kingdom of Westphalia
description: Napoleon officially organized this new German puppet state, placing his youngest brother, Jérôme Bonaparte, on its throne.

title: Maneuvers along the Elbe
description: Napoleon concentrated his forces around Düben, attempting to maneuver between the Allied armies of Blücher and Bernadotte to strike them individually before they could unite.

title: The Lines of Torres Vedras reached
description: British General Arthur Wellesley (Wellington) safely withdrew his Anglo-Portuguese army behind massive, secret defensive fortifications, completely halting the French invasion of Portugal.

title: Capture of Munich
description: French Marshal Bernadotte's corps entered and occupied Munich, securing Napoleon's flank and isolating Austrian General Mack at Ulm.

title: Murat's Feint at Landsberg
description: Marshal Joachim Murat executed a bold feint toward Landsberg, successfully deceiving the Allied Army of Bohemia into believing Napoleon intended to march south toward the Alps. This maneuver delayed the Allied advance and bought crucial time for Napoleon to consolidate his forces for the decisive Battle of Leipzig.

title: The Battle of Leipzig Ends
description: The massive 'Battle of the Nations' concluded as Allied forces stormed Leipzig, forcing a disastrous French retreat over the Elster River after a bridge was prematurely blown up.

title: Wellington Reaches the Duero
description: During his tactical retreat following the failed Siege of Burgos, British General Wellington successfully withdrew his Anglo-Portuguese army across the Duero River to escape pursuing French forces.

title: The Malet Coup Attempt
description: While Napoleon was retreating from Moscow, disgruntled French General Claude François de Malet launched a daring but failed coup d'état in Paris by spreading false rumors that the Emperor had died in Russia.

title: Coalition Advance on the Rhine
description: In the rapid aftermath of the Battle of Leipzig, Allied Coalition forces consolidated their grip on Germany and pushed westward, forcing the remnants of Napoleon's army to retreat toward the French border.

title: The Battle of Saguntum
description: French Marshal Suchet won a major victory against a Spanish relief army under General Blake, forcing the nearby fortress of Saguntum to surrender days later.

title: Allied Occupation of Hamburg
description: In the rapid aftermath of the Battle of Leipzig, Allied Coalition forces consolidated their grip on Germany and pushed westward, forcing the remnants of Napoleon's army to retreat toward the French border.

title: Battle of María
description: The Battle of María saw a small Spanish army led by Joaquín Blake y Joyes face an Imperial French corps under Louis Gabriel Suchet.

title: Battle of Valls
description: The Battle of Valls was fought on 25 February 1809, during the Peninsular War, between a French force under Laurent Gouvion Saint-Cyr and a Spanish force under Theodor von Reding.

title: Combat of Neresheim
description: The Battle of Neresheim took place on 17 October 1805. It ended in a victory for the French army commanded by Marshal Joachim Murat. The opposing force was the Austrian army of General Franz von Werneck. The action resulted in the capture of 1,350 men. Among the prisoners was General Rudolf von Sinzendorf. The French also captured 2 colours.

title: Surrender of Saguntum Castle
description: Following their defeat at the Battle of Saguntum the previous day, the Spanish garrison formally surrendered the ancient hilltop fortress to French Marshal Suchet.

title: The Skirmish at Medyn
description: Russian Cossacks intercepted and defeated a vanguard of French-allied Polish cavalry, forcing Napoleon to quickly reinforce his flank during the retreat from Moscow.

title: Battle of Teugen-Hausen
description: Also known as battle of Thann. The battle was fought on 19 April 1809 between the French III Corps led by Marshal Louis-Nicolas Davout and the Austrian III Armeekorps commanded by Prince Friedrich Franz Xaver of Hohenzollern-Hechingen. When the Austrians withdrew that evening, the French won a hard-fought victory over their opponents.

title: End of the Battle of Smoliani
description: On November 14th, the combat intensified as 5,000 of Victor's troops attacked and captured Smoliani. After this, however, the French attacking force suffered a reversal, being repulsed on the Russian right wing and then losing Smoliani to Wittgenstein's counterattack.

title: Battle of Epinal
description: The Crown Prince of Württemberg launched a three-column attack against General Rousseau's French forces, who successfully executed an orderly retreat to Charmes despite suffering heavy losses and 500 prisoners due to delayed Allied flank support from General Platoff.

title: Battle of Sehested
description: The Battle of Sehested was fought on 10 December 1813 during the Dano-Swedish War of 1813-1814 between a Danish army under Prince Frederik of Hesse and a Coalition force led by Ludwig von Wallmoden-Gimborn.

title: Archduke Charles is Born in Florence
description: Archduke Charles was born at the Pitti Palace, residence of his father, Leopold, then Grand Duke of Tuscany.

title: Karl Philipp, Prince of Schwarzenberg is Born in Vienna
description: Karl Philipp Schwarzenberg was born on April 15th 1771 at the Palais Schwarzenberg in Vienna, family residence since 1716.

title: Marshal Joseph Radetzky is Born in Třebnice, Sedlčany, Czech Republic
description: He was born on November 2nd 1766into a noble Bohemian military family of Czech origin at Chateau Třebnice (Trebnitz) near Sedlčany in Bohemia.

title: Karl Mack von Leiberich is Born
description: He was born on August 25th 1752 in Nennslingen, Principality of Ansbach.

title: Michael von Melas is Born
description: He was born in Radeln, Transylvania, (nowadays Roadeș, part of Bunești commune, Brașov County, Romania) on May 12th 1729 and joined the Austrian Army at age 17.

title: Birth of József Alvinczi
description: He was born on February 1st 1735 in Alvinc, now part of the village of Vințu de Jos, in the Transylvania region of Romania.

title: Birth of Johann I Joseph, Prince of Liechtenstein
description: He was born on June 26th 1760 in Vienna, Austria, at the Liechtenstein City Palace.

title: Birth of Frederick William, Duke of Brunswick-Wolfenbüttel
description: He was born on October 9th 1771 in Braunschweig, Duchy of Brunswick-Wolfenbüttel, Holy Roman Empire, at the Brunswick Palace.

title: First Battle of Algeciras
description: The First Battle of Algeciras was fought on 6 July 1801 between a Royal Navy squadron and a smaller French Navy squadron lying at anchor in the Spanish port of Algeciras during the Algeciras campaign of the War of the Second Coalition.

title: Second Battle of Algeciras
description: The Second Battle of Algeciras was fought on 12 July 1801 between a Royal Navy squadron and a smaller French Navy squadron lying at anchor in the Spanish port of Algeciras during the Algeciras campaign of the War of the Second Coalition.

title: Combat of Tollo
description: The combat of Tollo, also known as the Action of Utiel, took place on August 25, 1812, in Utiel (Valencia). Spanish forces led by General Pedro Villacampa defeated Napoleon's troops under General Maupoint, halting their advance towards Valencia and boosting the morale of the Spanish resistance.
